title: Tarenna littoralis
taxonomy: Tarenna littoralis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Tarenna and is native to tropical regions. Tarenna littoralis is classified as a type of shrub or small tree, often found in coastal areas and forests. The plant is further classified within the order Gentianales and the class Magnoliopsida. Tarenna littoralis is a member of the kingdom Plantae, indicating its status as a vascular plant.

intro: Tarenna littoralis, also known as the coastal Tarenna, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly found in tropical regions with rich vegetation. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Tarenna littoralis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for the plant's therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, which are commonly used to treat respiratory and skin-related ailments, as well as to promote overall well-being.
